output_debit_classifier.reason: Target purchased merchandise inventory on account and incurred various costs. Under IAS 2, the cost of inventory includes the purchase price plus costs necessary to bring the inventory to its present location and condition. The merchandise ($1,000,000), inbound freight to warehouse ($120,000), and shipping insurance ($30,000) are all capitalizable to inventory, totaling $1,150,000. Post-acquisition warehouse storage ($20,000) is a period cost that cannot be capitalized per IAS 2.16. Outbound delivery freight ($50,000) is a selling expense incurred after the inventory is ready for sale. Cash paid totals $220,000 for the four cash items. The merchandise purchase creates an accounts payable of $1,000,000.
